title: Fix crash on quadlet Mount bad option
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20104

<!--

-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). @vrothberg: once the present PR merges, I will cherry-pick it on top of v4.7 in a new PR and assign it to you.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20112#issuecomment-1732981607):

>/cherry-pick v4.7


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
NOTE that there is also https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20108 attempting to fix the same issue

@Luap99, I agree, the `Mount` code needs some attention.  Quadlet is parsing the code to 
* make sure the specified sources are mounted by adding a `RequiresMountsFor`
* transform a `.volume` mount source

Maybe function for parsing mounts (from Podman) should be used instead to make sure things are being parsed correctly.

> This code is broken and does not allow valid mount flag values! i.e. `--mount type=bind,src=/tmp,dst=/tmp,ro`

I think this works (see tests).
could we consider #20108 since it was opened first?

> > This code is broken and does not allow valid mount flag values! i.e. `--mount type=bind,src=/tmp,dst=/tmp,ro`
> 
> I think this works (see tests).

I don't see any tests for an option without `=`. This PR adds code which will error out if it does not contain an equal sign which means its breaks valid options and backwards compatibility. 
In general this parsing code is broken, this is not how we parse --mount, we cannot split by comma it has to use a proper CSV parser to handle the escaping of special chars.

please add this as test:
```diff
diff --git a/test/e2e/quadlet/mount.container b/test/e2e/quadlet/mount.container
index dae7be9f2..52b52d06b 100644
--- a/test/e2e/quadlet/mount.container
+++ b/test/e2e/quadlet/mount.container
@@ -8,6 +8,8 @@ Mount=type=bind,src=/path/on/host,dst=/path/in/container,relabel=shared
 Mount=type=bind,src=/path/on/host,dst=/path/in/container,relabel=shared,U=true
 ## assert-podman-args-key-val "--mount" "," "type=volume,source=vol1,destination=/path/in/container,ro=true"
 Mount=type=volume,source=vol1,destination=/path/in/container,ro=true
+## assert-podman-args-key-val "--mount" "," "type=volume,source=vol1,destination=/path/in/container,ro"
+Mount=type=volume,source=vol1,destination=/path/in/container,ro
 ## assert-podman-args-key-val "--mount" "," "type=volume,source=systemd-vol2,destination=/path/in/container,ro=true"
 ## assert-key-is "Unit" "Requires" "vol2-volume.service"
 ## assert-key-is "Unit" "After" "vol2-volume.service"
```

Which will fail with 
```
converting "mount.container": invalid mount option "ro", must contain an `=` sign
```
Removed parsing from quadlet, let Podman deal with the bogus params.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 1552ec28f8aa9f0812134e5ec67aaa3e94e46bb3. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

/hold
I've made a more elaborate fix in #20149
